Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Washington DC dbt Meetup
4.9
11 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Alex Welch, Head of Data @dbt Labs
Topic: BI Tools & Semantic Layer
When: June 27 @ 4pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!
This month we have Alex Welch, Head of Data at dbt Labs, to answer questions and discuss more about BI tools, analytics trends & the Semantic Layer!

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-merge channel in Slack to participate live!

Mon, Jun 3, 2024, 5:30 PM to Mon, Jun 3, 2024, 7:30 PM EDT

Excella

2300 Wilson Blvd Suite 600 · Arlington, VA

Washington DC area dbt Meetup

Hosted by Washington DC dbt Meetup

Washington DC dbt Meetup
4.9
11 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, but presentations can cover broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs

🏠Venue Host: Excella, 2300 Wilson Boulevard, Suite 600, Arlington, VA 22201

🍕Catering: Light bites & beverages

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

🗣️ Presentations:

"Scalable Model Generation with Jinja and Macros"
Vatsal Sanghavi (Senior Analytics Engineer, Datadog)
"Streamlining Development for Strive Health Data Analytics"
Matthew Temba (Chief Technology Officer, Strive Health)

Event details:

The doors open at 5:30pm for check-in and networking. Presentations begin at 6:00 and there will be additional networking time until 7:30.

Refreshments will be provided.

Directions:
The Excella office is located near the Court House Metro station on the Orange line.

Parking on premise at 2300 Wilson Blvd is extremely limited. If you are unable to park in the building's garage or on the street near the building, we suggest the following parking garages, which are within close walking distance:

2300 Clarendon Boulevard
2101 Wilson Boulevard

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-dcmetro channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more

Presenting:

From Zero to Hero with dbt

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

Our partner NuAxis have generously offered the use of their brand new offices for this event.
Location: 1919 Gallows Road, Suite 610, Vienna, VA 22182

🏠Venue Host: NuAxis Innovations
🍕Catering: Pizza and Beer and Softdrinks
🤝Organizer: Vivanti

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda

17:30 - 18:00 | Check in/Registration (30 min)
18:00 - 18:05 | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
18:05 - 18:35 | Presentation (15-20 min)
18:35 - 18:45 | Q&A (5 min)
18:45 - 18:50 | Closing Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
18:50 - 19:30 | Networking reception

Description: Emily will talk through her journey with dbt from first contact to making dbt her favourite data wrangling tool.

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 5:30pm. Presentations begin at 6:05pm
Food and refreshments will be provided.
If you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-dcmetro channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more
Speaker
Emily Charde

Emily brings a strong background in data science and analytics with a passion for finding new ways to conceptualize data questions. She enjoys cleaning and organizing data as much as analyzing the end results to find the story within the data. She loves working with new technologies and is excited to share them with others, but believes that the best solution is one that the client is comfortable with and will actually use.

520 Mill St NE

520 Mill St NE · Vienna, VA

Washington DC - Casual Get Together - May 11th

Hosted by Mike W.

Washington DC dbt Meetup
4.9
11 reviews
Details

Hey DC People, for those who are interested [@james.hunt](https://getdbt.slack.com/team/U047L5ZNZ8Q) and I will be hosting a get together at the Caboose Brewing Co & Tavern (the one in Tysons) on May 11th from 6pm. This is not a formal Meetup, just a casual opportunity to talk data. James and I have been doing some interesting work around how to make use of data sourced from Public and Private data sets that we have fond captivating enough to want to share. If you’ve anything else you’d like to talk about bring it along, we’d love to see you and chat. (edited)

18:45 - 18:50 | Closing Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
18:50 - 19:30 | Networking reception

Description: James brings a tale from the field using dbt macros to do large scale validation of data sets. He talks about the need to look at all of the data holistically instead of only spot checking.

Speaker bio: A lifelong learner and self-professed breaker of things, James has a long and storied career of figuring out some of the thorniest problems of scale, automation, and reliability when it comes to data and code.

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 5:30pm. Presentations begin at 6:05pm
Food and refreshments will be provided.
If you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

----------------------------------

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/
